title: lactuca quercina subsp. stricta
intro: Lactuca quercina subsp. stricta is a subspecies of the wild lettuce plant, native to Europe and Asia, known for its tall, slender stems and narrow, pointed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of Lactuca quercina subsp. stricta include reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in tinctures, and can be consumed as a tea, decoction, or added to salves and creams for topical application.
